title: jacea phrygia
intro: Jacea phrygia, also known as Fraxinus ornus, is a deciduous tree native to Europe and Western Asia, belonging to the Oleaceae family. The bark, leaves, and flowers of the plant have been used in traditional medicine for their medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antiseptic, and antiviral effects. The health benefits of Jacea phrygia include treating fever, reducing inflammation, and easing respiratory issues, as well as acting as a natural diuretic and antiseptic. In herbal preparations, the plant's bark and leaves are typically dried and used to make teas, infusions, and tinctures that can be consumed to harness its medicinal properties.
